channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Kane scores twice as England come from behind to reach last 16
item.description: Harry Kane scores twice as England come from behind to beat DR Congo in a tense World Cup encounter in Atlanta, and set up a last-16 meeting with Mexico.

item.title: Spurs agree deal worth up to £100m for Newcastle's Tonali
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur have agreed a deal worth up to £100m with Newcastle United for midfielder Sandro Tonali.

item.title: England sneak through - but who had a nightmare start? Player ratings
item.description: BBC Sport England reporter Alex Howell rates the players after the World Cup comeback win over DR Congo - plus have your say.

item.title: 'That was a rocket' - Kane scores winner for England
item.description: A "rocket" of a strike from Harry Kane gives England a late lead against DR Congo in Atlanta.

item.title: England into last 16 - what is their path to the final?
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the final after progressing the last 16

item.title: Should England have had a penalty for challenge on Kane?
item.description: Should England have been awarded a penalty in their World Cup last-32 tie against DR Congo?

item.title: Nottingham Forest set to replace Pereira with Glasner
item.description: Vitor Pereira is poised to leave Nottingham Forest with the club ready to appoint former Crystal Palace boss Oliver Glasner as his successor.

item.title: No signings, no sales - what is happening at Manchester United?
item.description: Manchester United have missed out on a second midfield target in Mateus Fernandes but are refusing to panic even though they are yet to sign any players this summer.

item.title: Brighton agree club record £46m Vuskovic deal
item.description: Brighton have third bid accepted by Tottenham and will pay club record £46m for highly-rated young defender Luka Vuskovic

item.title: Chelsea sign Italy defender Palestra for £47m
item.description: Right-back Marco Palestra, 21, becomes Xabi Alonso's first signing as Chelsea manager.

item.title: Win over DR Congo 'our best game of the tournament' - Kane
item.description: England captain Harry Kane believes the Three Lions' attacking play against DR Congo was the best they have shown so far in the World Cup after their 2-1 win in Atlanta.

item.title: Energy and team spirit at highest level - Tuchel
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel praises his sides team spirit as they come from behind to beat DR Congo 2-1 and advance to the round of 16.

item.title: Relief for England as Kane heads in equalising goal against DR Congo
item.description: Harry Kane heads in from Anthony Gordon's cross as England equalise against DR Congo in their round of 32 game in Atlanta.

item.title: All the angles of Kane's brilliant winner
item.description: All the angles of Harry Kane's winning goal against DR Congo for England in their round of 32 game in Atlanta.

item.title: 'He is ridiculous' - Rice praise for match-winner Kane
item.description: Declan Rice describes England captain Harry Kane as "ridiculous" after he scores twice during their World Cup victory over DR Congo in Atlanta.

item.title: Could Rangers make Ferguson marquee signing?
item.description: BBC Scotland looks at what Bologna captain Lewis Ferguson would offer to a club where his family are so deeply ingrained.

item.title: Four die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
item.description: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico's capital on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.

item.title: Hull avoid points deduction by selling players
item.description: Hull City avoided the threat of a points deduction in the Premier League by selling two players hours before Tuesday's deadline.

item.title: Concern over rise in online racist abuse at World Cup
item.description: The Word Cup has suffered a 'significant increase' in the most serious examples of racist abuse online, Fifa's social media protection service (SMPS) has found.

item.title: Has football fever taken hold in the US?
item.description: With the World Cup now three weeks old, BBC Sport's reporters in the US look at how their perceptions have changed.

item.title: The small Basque club that produced Arteta, Alonso and Iraola
item.description: The Arsenal, Chelsea and Liverpool managers all began their football journeys in the Basque region

item.title: United, free and brilliant - can anyone stop France masterclass?
item.description: France boss Didier Deschamps urges caution despite watching his side dismantle Sweden to leave Les Bleus fans dreaming of winning the World Cup.

item.title: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup knockout games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: Quiz: Name every team England have played in World Cup knockouts
item.description: England and Thomas Tuchel will want to match the achievements of the nation's famous side that lifted the World Cup trophy in 1966. Can you name every side they have ever faced in the knockouts?

item.title: PSG set Barcola price - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Paris St-Germain set their price for Bradley Barcola, Chelsea want Bournemouth striker Eli Junior Kroupi, Ayyoub Bouaddi is attracting interest, plus more.

item.title: Defying logic - how Haaland could end up with 260 international goals
item.description: Erling Haaland delivers on the biggest stage yet for Norway - but just how many goals could he end up with in international football?

item.title: Midfielder Neil joins Rangers from Sunderland
item.description: Dan Neil expects "a very similar feeling" of trying to meet expectations after joining Rangers from Sunderland on a three-year deal.

item.title: Hincapie becomes second player sent off for covering mouth
item.description: Ecuador defender Piero Hincapie became the second player at the World Cup to be dismissed for covering his mouth while confronting an opponent.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 24
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Gordon was 'already celebrating' before Kane had scored the winner
item.description: Anthony Gordon praises England's "togetherness" in their 2-1 victory over DR Congo in their round of 32 match in Atlanta.

item.title: Pundits split on Kane penalty claim
item.description: Micah Richards, Joe Hart, Wayne Rooney and Darren Cann debate whether England should have had a penalty after Harry Kane went down under a challenge from DR Congo goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi-Nzau.

item.title: 'Wonderful finish' - Cipenga gives DR Congo shock lead against England
item.description: Brian Cipenga gives DR Congo an early lead against England during their World Cup last-32 match in Atlanta.

item.title: Should England have been awarded a penalty?
item.description: England's appeals for a penalty are waved away after DR Congo goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi appears to bring down Harry Kane.

item.title: 'What a save!' - Rashford's effort blocked on line by Wan-Bissaka
item.description: Marcus Rashford's effort is cleared off the line by Aaron Wan-Bissaka as England go close to an equaliser against DR Congo in their round of 32 game in Atlanta.

item.title: Mexico set up potential England tie with win over 10-man Ecuador
item.description: Mexico cruise into the last 16 of the World Cup with a comfortable victory over Ecuador in a weather-delayed contest at the Azteca Stadium.

item.title: Mbappe scores twice as France dominate Sweden
item.description: Kylian Mbappe becomes the all-time leading goalscorer in World Cup knockout matches with after scoring twice as France crusie past Sweden 3-0 to set up a last-16 tie with Paraguay.

item.title: Fifa explains decision to rule out Germany goal
item.description: Fifa says coaches and players were told before the World Cup referees will punish the type of foul that saw Germany's extra-time goal against Paraguay ruled out.

item.title: Beccacece reign as Ecuador manager over after exit
item.description: Ecuador manager Sebastian Beccacece says he is leaving his role following his side's World Cup exit against Mexico.

item.title: Egypt optimistic Salah will be fit to face Australia
item.description: Sources inside the Egypt camp say Mohamed Salah is winning his race to overcome a hamstring injury and play in Friday's last-32 game in Dallas.

item.title: Spurs agree £85m deal for West Ham's Fernandes
item.description: Tottenham agree a deal to sign West Ham's Mateus Fernandes as Manchester United pull out of the race for midfielder.

item.title: Villa sign Denmark striker Vangsgaard
item.description: Amalie Vangsgaard says she is "very excited for this new adventure" after joining Aston Villa from Juventus.

item.title: Brighton sign Leeds defender Struijk for about £20m
item.description: Brighton sign Leeds United defender Pascal Struijk for an undisclosed fee understood to be about £20m.

item.title: The Paraguay hero who sold his kit when his son was born
item.description: Orlando Gill has become Paraguay's World Cup hero but was in football obscurity 18 months ago and had to sell his kit to support his family after the premature birth of his son.

item.title: Mexico rampant at fortress Azteca - and England could be next
item.description: Mexico have won all four games and not conceded a goal at this World Cup. Their last-16 opponents must also contend with playing at the Azteca Stadium.

item.title: Koeman resigns, while Netherlands report racist abuse
item.description: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties.

item.title: Arsenal consider Barcola as alternative to Rogers
item.description: Arsenal are expected to step up their interest in Paris Saint-Germain's Bradley Barcola this week after receiving encouragement that the forward could be available this summer.

item.title: Villa among four Premier League clubs fined by Uefa over squad-cost rules
item.description: Aston Villa have been fined 22.5m euros (£19.4m) by Uefa for a "significant breach" of its squad-cost rule for 2025.

item.title: Coventry boss Lampard signs new contract to 2029
item.description: Coventry City manager Frank Lampard signs a new contract until 2029 before the club's first Premier League campaign in 25 years.

item.title: 45 minutes from Brazil 'humiliation' - but 'Crafty Carlo' does it again
item.description: Half-time in Houston and Brazil's players trudged off facing another early World Cup exit. But we should have known better than to doubt Carlo Ancelotti.

item.title: How to win a World Cup penalty shootout
item.description: We have a look at what can be learned from every World Cup penalty taken in a shootout ever.

item.title: England last-32 tie on BBC - see full schedule
item.description: England's World Cup last-32 game against DR Congo will be shown live on BBC One and BBC iPlayer, with all 16 first knockout round ties covered across BBC Sport.

item.title: Pampered, protected & primed - so why didn't Scotland players perform?
item.description: Scotland's players got everything they asked for at this World Cup. So why did they not perform on the pitch in the US?

item.title: Best team, moments, matches and players of World Cup group stage
item.description: As the World Cup group stages come to an end, BBC Sport reporters pick their highlights so far.

item.title: 'Bye-bye, Scotland' - Clarke's leaving letter to Tartan Army
item.description: Steve Clarke's letter to the Scotland fans in full following his departure as head coach.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: What is BBC's 3D Live Match Experience and how does it work?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the unique way of following matches during the 2026 World Cup via the BBC

item.title: Why Saudi Arabia and Iraq flags are not laid down at World Cup
item.description: The flags of Saudi Arabia and Iraq are not laid on the ground during World Cup pre-match ceremonies because they include religious wording.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: Rangers buy Hull goalkeeper Pandur for £6m - what will he bring?
item.description: Rangers sign Croatian goalkeeper Ivor Pandur from Hull City for £6m - what will he bring to Ibrox?

item.title: Why new Scotland head coach has tricky task
item.description: Steve Clarke's successor as Scotland head coach may have a tricky task to sustain the national side's success over the long term.

item.title: Might set-piece coach lured from Hearts be Celtic's key signing?
item.description: BBC Scotland crunches the numbers behind Ross Grant's set-piece impact at Hearts and looks at what he could bring to Celtic.

item.title: Harsh reality behind Scotland fans' calls for Moyes & Postecoglou
item.description: Some Scotland fans are dreaming of David Moyes or Ange Postecoglou replacing Steve Clarke, but could either man be a realistic prospect?

item.title: Clarke says standing down 'easy' decision after World Cup exit
item.description: Steve Clarke says it was an easy decision to step down as Scotland head coach because he always planned to depart if they did not reach the knockout stage.

item.title: Spurs sign Norway keeper Panengstuen from SK Brann
item.description: Norwegian international goalkeeper Selma Panengstuen says it is a "dream" move to join Women's Super League side Tottenham.

item.title: Coach called Cusack a 'psycho', inquest told
item.description: A team-mate tells an inquest Maddy Cusack was "anxious" about Jonathan Morgan joining Sheffield United.

item.title: Kerr joins NWSL side Gotham FC
item.description: Australian international Sam Kerr has joined Gotham FC on a four-year deal following her departure from Chelsea.

item.title: Pattinson signs new deal with London City Lionesses
item.description: London City Lionesses has announced England defender Poppy Pattinson has extended her contract until summer 2029.

item.title: Football triplets make historic debut for Guernsey
item.description: A set of 16-year-old triplets make their debut for Guernsey as the island side finishes third in the Cherry Godfrey Cup.

item.title: Double Ballon d'Or winner Putellas to join London City Lionesses
item.description: Two-time Ballon d'Or winner Alexia Putellas decides to move to London City Lionesses after leaving Barcelona.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
